content.problemStatement:
The greatest common divisor (GCD) of a sequence is the greatest positive integer which divides each element of this sequence.

You are given a sequence AAA of positive integers with size NNN. You are allowed to delete up to N−1N-1N−1 elements from this sequence. (I.e., you may delete any number of elements, including zero, as long as the resulting sequence is non-empty.)

Please find the minimum number of elements which have to be deleted so that the GCD of the resulting sequence would be equal to 111, or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of elements to delete, or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to make the GCD equal to 111.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The GCD is already equal to 111, so the answer is 000.
Example case 2: It is impossible to make the GCD equal to 111, so the answer is −1-1−1.
